heroHeadline: Vatican Responds as Trump-Pope Diplomatic Feud Continues
heroSubtitle: PBS reports widespread financial strain among US farmers as the spring planting season begins, with agricultural producers hit simultaneously by Trump's broad tariff regime raising input costs and the Iran war's energy shock driving diesel and fertilizer prices sharply higher. The compounding pressure is forcing some farm operators to delay planting decisions and struggle to secure operating loans — adding a significant rural economic dimension to the political fallout from the ongoing US-Iran conflict.
footerNote: Data sourced from White House, Federal Register, executive orders, court records, Al Jazeera, CNN, Reuters, AP, NPR, Bloomberg, CNBC, Axios, NBC News, CBS News, Gulf News, The National, Newsweek, Nate Silver/Silver Bulletin, YouGov/Economist, Fox News, BLS, Peterson Institute for International Economics, Tax Foundation, CFR, EIA, Treasury, Goldman Sachs, Federal Reserve, OECD, Cato Institute, Penn Wharton Budget Model.
